content.title: What is C?
content.content:
C is a basic but strong computer language made in 1972. It helps you tell the computer exactly what to do, step by step.

It's used for making many types of programs, like parts of operating systems (like Windows or Linux) and software that needs to run very fast.

content.title: Why learn C?
content.content:
Learning C is great because:

  1. It helps you understand how computers work at a basic level.
  2. Many other languages (like C++, Java, and Python) were inspired by C, so learning C makes learning them easier.
  3. It's still used a lot in special areas like making operating systems and programs for small devices.

sections.title: Basic Structure of a C Program

content.title: How a C program looks
content.content:
A simple C program has a few main parts:

  1. Include files: These bring in ready-made code that helps your program do things, like printing text.
  2. The main part (main function): This is where your program starts running. Every C program must have this.
  3. Code inside main: Here you write the steps you want the computer to follow.

Think of it like writing instructions for a robot.

content.title: Example program
What are variables?
content.content: Imagine you have boxes to store different kinds of things. In programming, variables are like these boxes. A variable is a name you give to a storage area in the computer's memory. You use it to hold information (data) that your program needs, like numbers or text.

content.title: Data types in C
content.content:
C needs to know what kind of data you want to store in your variable box. This is called a 'data type'.

Common data types in C:

  • int: For whole numbers (like 5, -10, 1000)
  • float: For numbers with decimal points (like 3.14, 0.01, -2.5)
  • char: For single letters or symbols (like 'A', 'b', '7', '$')

There are other types too, but these are the basics.
